cc_source_mapping_report.preserves: all v2 metadata, structural notes, tier classifications, sunroad examples
cc_source_mapping_report.adds: source field on each Tier A input + cc_note field on Tier X inputs
cc_source_mapping_report.headline_finding: Approximately 65% of Tier A cells (78 of 119) have NO current codebase source — the underlying data is not extracted by any pipeline. The blocker for Milestone 1 is NOT 'legacy vs graph pipeline access' (the user's a-priori concern); it is 'large categories of deal data are not extracted at all'. Recommend re-scoping Milestone 1 around the data that IS available, with the rest flagged as Tier C (red highlight) for the underwriter to fill manually until extraction expands.

in_scope_legacy_POST_handler.PropertyMetadata: graph-pipeline-adjacent, built in legacy POST at line 988 via extractPropertyMetadata. Available before populateTemplate runs (line 1015). Carries: propertyName, propertySubtype, address, city, state, zip, county, msa, submarket, yearBuilt, yearRenovated, buildingClass, totalSquareFeet, totalUnits, totalRooms, totalPads, occupancyPhysical, occupancyEconomic, ownershipInterest, numberOfBuildings
in_scope_legacy_POST_handler.UnderwritingModel: legacy @cre/shared. pipeline.uwModel. Carries: income.{grossPotentialRent, vacancyLoss, concessions, otherIncome, EGI}, expenses.{realEstateTaxes, insurance, utilities, repairsAndMaintenance, management, generalAndAdmin, payroll, replacementReserves, totalExpenses}, netOperatingIncome, capRate, impliedValue, loanAmount, interestRate, amortizationYears, termYears, loanDetails.{rateType, ioMonths, amortizationMonths, termMonths, paymentFrequency, prepaymentTerms, originationDate}
in_scope_legacy_POST_handler.UnderwritingModel_per_source: legacy. pipeline.uwModelFromAsr (T-12-leaning), pipeline.uwModelFromSeller (Seller-UW column). Each has same shape as UnderwritingModel — provides the per-source columns the Operating History sheet needs
in_scope_legacy_POST_handler.legacy_ExtractionResult: @cre/shared (NOT @cre/contracts). pipeline.extractionResult. Carries: fields[CoreFieldName].value/confidence (5 numeric: noi, loanAmount, interestRate, capRate, propertyValue), descriptors[DescriptorFieldName].value (8 strings: propertyName, street, city, state, zip, propertyType, borrowerName, sponsorName), structural[StructuralFieldName].value (7 numerics: loanTermMonths, amortizationMonths, ioMonths, yearBuilt, totalSquareFeet, units, occupancy)
in_scope_legacy_POST_handler.RentRoll: graph-pipeline-adjacent. Resolved in legacy POST at line 949-981 via parseRentRollXlsx | extractRentRollFromDocument. Carries tenant-level rows for the Rent Roll sheet
in_scope_legacy_POST_handler.Analysis_top_level: legacy @cre/shared. Analysis row from sqlite-store. Carries: id (UUID, not a deal number), name (analysis name as entered by user), assetType (lowercase user pick), createdAt, updatedAt
in_scope_legacy_POST_handler.AssetType: asset class string from upload form (user picks). PascalCase via @cre/contracts.AssetType OR lowercase via @cre/shared.AssetType depending on call site.

NOT_in_scope_legacy_POST_handler_today.contract_ExtractionResult: @cre/contracts. Carries structured rentRoll/t12/sellerUw/appraisal/asr/loanTerms with line-item line items. Built by the graph-pipeline ingest path (ingestExtractionResult), NEVER built in the legacy POST. To use this in the populator would require either (a) running ingestExtractionResult inline or (b) the new-spine populator from legacy-reduction-plan §7
NOT_in_scope_legacy_POST_handler_today.AdjustedInputs: @cre/contracts. Post-judgment line items. Available only via the graph pipeline.
NOT_in_scope_legacy_POST_handler_today.AssetProfile: @cre/contracts. propertyType + businessPlan + marketLiquidity. Trivial; could be derived inline if needed
NOT_in_scope_legacy_POST_handler_today.NarrativeFacts: @cre/contracts. Mostly null in current implementation; not useful for template population today
NOT_in_scope_legacy_POST_handler_today.StressOutputs: @cre/contracts. The legacy POST has pipeline.stressScenarios (legacy StressScenario[]) — DIFFERENT shape but same conceptual data. Adequate substitute for populator purposes.

biggest_missing_buckets.why_missing: No current extraction captures legal/structural loan terms beyond loanAmount/interestRate/term/amortization/IO. The build-and-ingest route's `loanTerms` form field carries 5 numeric fields only (loanAmount, interestRate, amortization, IO, maturityDate). None of the Y/N flags or descriptive terms are extracted.
biggest_missing_buckets.proposed_action: (a) Add legal-loan-terms section to form input + extraction OR (b) Tier C downgrade for Milestone 1 (red highlight, underwriter fills). Recommend (b) for Milestone 1; (a) is a separate batch.

biggest_missing_buckets.why_missing: No sources & uses extraction in current pipeline. Issue #35 lists 'Sources & uses structured extraction' as a known engine gap (item 10 — unlocks P-II-3 handbook principle).
biggest_missing_buckets.proposed_action: Tier C downgrade for Milestone 1. Issue #35 will eventually surface this data via the engine extension path.

biggest_missing_buckets.cells: All Operating History columns B/D/F line items (6 line items × 3 prior years ≈ 30 cells, plus reimbursements + occupancy per period ≈ 36 total)
biggest_missing_buckets.why_missing: Issue #38 (open) explicitly tracks this: 'Extract per-period pro-forma arrays from seller UW models'. Current extraction is T-12 only (single period via uwModelFromAsr) + seller-UW column (single period via uwModelFromSeller). No multi-year historical array.
biggest_missing_buckets.proposed_action: Tier C downgrade for prior-year columns until Issue #38 ships. T-12 column H + Seller-UW column L are populatable from existing data.

cells: All Operating History col J line-item rows (J9 GPR, J11 Bad Debt, J14 Other Income, J15 Reimbursements, J22 G&A, J24 R&M, J25 Utilities, J26 Other OpEx, J30 Mgmt Fee, J31 Property Taxes, J32 Insurance)
biggest_missing_buckets.why_missing: @cre/contracts.AppraisalExtraction has only {valueConclusion, capRate, methodology} — no expense line items. The appraisal extractor doesn't parse the appraisal's operating-statement projections.
biggest_missing_buckets.proposed_action: Tier C downgrade for Milestone 1. Future appraisal extractor expansion would add an income/expense block to AppraisalExtraction.

cells: E18 Prior CMBS Balance Before Disposition
C19 Prior CMBS Trough NCF Year
D19 Prior CMBS Trough NCF Amount
biggest_missing_buckets.why_missing: No CMBS database in the codebase. Would require integration with an external CMBS data source (Trepp / Intex / rating-agency presale lookups).
biggest_missing_buckets.proposed_action: Tier C for Milestone 1. Possible Milestone 3 web-research tier.

biggest_missing_buckets.why_missing: PropertyMetadata carries identity + size + ownershipInterest + numberOfBuildings, but no zoning, parking, stories, land area, ground-lease details, or clear height (industrial-specific).
biggest_missing_buckets.proposed_action: Either (a) extend PropertyMetadata contract + extractor OR (b) Tier C for Milestone 1. Many of these are NAP for non-applicable asset classes; the populator should write 'NAP' rather than blank for fields that don't apply.

biggest_missing_buckets.why_missing: Securitization pool name and loan originator are not extracted today. Pool comes from CMBS docs (often unknown pre-securitization); Originator from loan docs (may be inferable but not captured).
biggest_missing_buckets.proposed_action: Reclassify to Tier C for Milestone 1.

what_IS_well_mapped.property_identity_and_address_block: 13 cells in Property & Loan Summary (E3-K8) all map cleanly to PropertyMetadata.* — this block of the template will populate well
what_IS_well_mapped.core_loan_terms_minimum: 5 cells (D13 originalBalance / C13 noteDate / E15 termMonths / E17 ioMonths / D18 coupon) map cleanly to UnderwritingModel.{loanAmount, loanDetails.originationDate, loanDetails.termMonths, loanDetails.ioMonths, interestRate}
what_IS_well_mapped.T_12_operating_history_column_H: ~9 line-item cells in Operating History col H map to UnderwritingModel income/expenses sections (or pipeline.uwModelFromAsr.* for the ASR-specific projection)
what_IS_well_mapped.rent_roll: Rent Roll sheet populates from pipeline.rentRoll via existing populator (handled by populateRentRollTab in template-engine.service.ts:914)
what_IS_well_mapped.subtype_and_class: PropertyMetadata.propertySubtype → H3 in Property Detail - Comm; PropertyMetadata.buildingClass → K6 P&L Summary AND C15 Property Detail (duplicate field)
what_IS_well_mapped.appraisal_value_only: I11 Appraisal Value maps to ExtractionResult.fields.propertyValue.value (legacy bag) OR UnderwritingModel.impliedValue. Note: the value/cap/methodology trio IS extracted; only the LINE-ITEM appraisal projections are not.

tier_X_resolutions.v3_recommendation: Confirm as template machinery; remove from input registry. Sunroad value = 1 is a constant for the map embed system, not a deal input.
tier_X_resolutions.action: Strike from input list during cleanup.

tier_X_resolutions.v3_recommendation: 45108 = 2023-07-08 in Excel date serial. Likely a 'map last refreshed' timestamp constant, NOT a deal input.
tier_X_resolutions.action: Strike from input list during cleanup.

tier_X_resolutions.v3_recommendation: Likely label drift between blank template and Sunroad. Inspect canonical template at K85; if it's a label cell, remove from registry. If it's an input intended for hotel/MF occupancy, classify as Tier A with source = PropertyMetadata.occupancyPhysical or rentRoll.summary.occupiedUnits.
tier_X_resolutions.action: Inspect Blank_UW_Template_v2.xlsm at K85 during cleanup.

tier_X_resolutions.v3_recommendation: Sunroad has 'null' value here. Likely a label cell, not an input. Inspect canonical template at E55.
tier_X_resolutions.action: Inspect during cleanup; if a label, strike from registry.

policy_decisions_to_make_before_M1_codes: Q1: For MISSING_FROM_CODEBASE cells — write 'NAP' (Not Applicable), write blank with red highlight, or skip the cell entirely? Recommend: red-highlight blank for all 'missing' cells per the registry's tier_C convention; reserve 'NAP' strictly for cross-asset-class non-applicability (e.g., clear height for office).

Q2: For DERIVED cells that depend on Tier C inputs (M2 Deal Title depends on H2 Deal Control Number which is Tier C) — populate with placeholder OR leave blank? Recommend: leave blank since the template formula M2 already pulls from H2 when H2 is filled.

Q3: M2 Cover Page reclassification — the registry has it as Tier A but the cell IS a formula in the live template (per `cell_counts.formula: 17` includes M2). Recommend: reclassify M2 from Tier A to FORMULA (i.e., don't write it; the live template formula handles derivation from H2 + Property Name automatically once H2 is filled).

Q4: Should the populator have a graph-pipeline-bridge mode for cells that are MISSING_FROM_CODEBASE today but available in @cre/contracts (e.g., ExtractionResult.appraisal.valueConclusion vs the legacy fields.propertyValue.value)? Recommend: NO for Milestone 1 — use legacy POST records only. Revisit when the new-spine populator from legacy-reduction-plan §7 ships.

registry_metadata.purpose: Input-cell registry for populate-the-blank-underwriting feature (Milestone 1). Identifies which template cells are INPUTS (the underwriter or system fills) vs FORMULAS (computed) vs LABELS (static text) vs BLANK. The populator writes ONLY into input cells, preserving the live financial model.

derived_from.example_property: Sunroad Centrum, San Diego CA, Class A, 274,758 SF, $82.46M loan
derived_from.canonical_template_note: Eightfold scrub completed manually in Excel; this is the cleaned template for the populator.

tier_classification.A_extraction: Auto-populate from ASR / loan docs / cash flow / rent roll uploads. Blue text in output.
tier_classification.B_judgment: Auto-populate via LLM + handbook judgment. Yellow background in output. NOT in Milestone 1 scope.
tier_classification.C_manual: Leave blank for underwriter. Red highlight in output to draw the eye.
tier_classification.X_investigate: Unclear — needs CC review during source mapping.

visual_scheme.tier_A_color: blue text (RGB 0,0,255) — industry-standard hardcoded input convention
visual_scheme.tier_C_color: red highlight (cell fill or border) — draw the eye to required manual entry

registry_metadata.asset_class_scope: Office/Retail/Industrial (via Sunroad example). MF/SS/MHP and Hotel sheets present in template but no example data; deferred to v1.5.
deferred: MF/SS/MHP and Hotel asset-class registries — v1.5 once example files are available

Cover Page.purpose: Printed cover with deal title, sponsor, originator, photos, maps.
Cover Page.tier_summary: 8 inputs: 4 Tier A, 0 Tier B, 4 Tier C, 0 X.

inputs.label: Deal Control Number
inputs.notes: Internal deal sequence number. The M2 formula uses this to generate the cover title "001- {PropertyName}". Per-deal manual entry (red highlight) OR system-assigned from internal deal numbering. CC to decide source during integration.

inputs.label: Deal title / Loan Purpose
inputs.notes: Formatted as `{dealNumber}- {propertyName}`. System-derived from internal deal numbering + property name.
inputs.source: DERIVED: `${Deal_Control_Number from H2 zero-padded to 3 digits}- ${PropertyMetadata.propertyName}`
inputs.cc_note: DERIVED with caveats. (a) Depends on H2 Deal_Control_Number which is itself Tier C (per-deal manual or system-assigned — unresolved). (b) The cell IS a formula in the live template (M2 counts toward cell_counts.formula:17 on Cover Page). Recommend reclassify to FORMULA, leave the template's own formula to derive M2 once H2 is filled. If we DO want to overwrite the formula with a hardcoded string, only write when both Deal_Control_Number AND PropertyMetadata.propertyName are available; otherwise leave the template formula intact.

inputs.label: Pool
inputs.notes: Securitization pool name from loan docs / CMBS docs. May be blank if not yet securitized.
inputs.cc_note: No current extraction captures CMBS pool name. Reclassify to Tier C for Milestone 1.

inputs.label: Underwriting Firm
inputs.notes: Re-entered per-deal per user direction. Leave blank, red highlight.

inputs.label: Underwriter
inputs.notes: Re-entered per-deal per user direction. Leave blank, red highlight.

inputs.label: Sponsor
inputs.notes: Sponsor entity name from ASR.

inputs.label: Originator
inputs.notes: Loan originator from loan docs.
inputs.cc_note: Loan originator (e.g., GSMC, Wells, MS-BAML) is not extracted by any current pipeline. Reclassify to Tier C for Milestone 1.

inputs.label: Prepared for
inputs.notes: Per user decision during template cleanup: this is a per-deal Tier C input (red highlight). The recipient the underwriting is being prepared for. Now blank in cleaned template.

Cover Page.non_input_notes: 5 photo placeholders (N3:O7) labeled Cover 1/Cover 2/Map 1/Map 2/Map. Reference image files at network path. Tier C, manual upload.

Property & Loan Summary.purpose: Central deal summary. Property description, loan terms, sources & uses, capital stack, history, annual cash flow summary, rent roll summary.
Property & Loan Summary.tier_summary: 44 inputs: 41 Tier A, 0 Tier B, 0 Tier C, 3 X.

inputs.label: Property Name
inputs.notes: Property name from ASR header

inputs.label: Property Type / Subtype
inputs.notes: Asset class — user picks at upload
inputs.source: Analysis.assetType (legacy @cre/shared.AssetType, lowercase — needs display-cased conversion to 'Office'/'Retail'/'Industrial' etc. via @cre/contracts.ASSET_TYPE_LABELS or inline case-fold)

inputs.label: Address
inputs.notes: Street address from ASR

inputs.label: Map factor (template machinery)
inputs.notes: Likely a template/layout control. NOT user-facing input. Investigate during cleanup.
inputs.cc_note: Resolved (recon): template machinery constant. Strike from input registry.

inputs.label: Map factor (template machinery)
inputs.notes: Likely a date serial (45108 = 2023-07-08). Template machinery. Investigate.
inputs.cc_note: Resolved (recon): Excel date serial 45108 = 2023-07-08, likely a 'map last refreshed' constant. Strike from input registry.

inputs.label: City
inputs.notes: City from ASR

inputs.label: Net Rentable Area / building size
inputs.notes: NRA in SF (Office/Retail/Industrial) or units (MF) or rooms (Hotel) from ASR.
inputs.source: DERIVED: dispatch by Analysis.assetType — Office/Retail/Industrial/SelfStorage/MixedUse → PropertyMetadata.totalSquareFeet; Multifamily → PropertyMetadata.totalUnits; Hotel → PropertyMetadata.totalRooms; MHC → PropertyMetadata.totalPads
inputs.cc_note: Asset-class dispatch logic at populator. PropertyMetadata carries all four measure fields; populator picks the one for the active asset type.

inputs.label: State
inputs.notes: 2-letter state from ASR

inputs.label: ZIP
inputs.notes: ZIP from ASR

inputs.label: Building Class
inputs.notes: Building class A/B/C from ASR. Duplicates Property Detail - Comm!C15.

inputs.label: County
inputs.notes: County from ASR

inputs.label: Year Built
inputs.notes: Year built from ASR

inputs.label: Year Renovated
inputs.notes: Year renovated from ASR. May be blank for unrenovated properties.

inputs.label: Submarket / MSA
inputs.notes: NOTE: cell labeled 'Submarket / MSA' but Sunroad has SUBMARKET here. Named range is MSA but data is submarket.
inputs.cc_note: Source field reflects the DATA semantics (submarket), not the named range label. PropertyMetadata also carries .msa separately if a true MSA value is needed elsewhere — confirm template intent during integration.

inputs.label: Ownership Interest
inputs.notes: Fee / Leasehold / Fee + Leasehold from ASR

inputs.label: Release Provisions (Y/N)
inputs.notes: Y/N flag from loan docs
inputs.cc_note: Loan structural Y/N flags are not extracted. Reclassify to Tier C.

inputs.label: Cutoff Date
inputs.notes: Loan cutoff date from loan docs
inputs.cc_note: Cutoff date (the date the loan was cut for securitization) is not extracted. ExtractionResult.loanTerms.maturityDate is the only loan date captured. Reclassify to Tier C.

inputs.label: Total Current Balance
inputs.notes: Current UPB from loan docs / servicer report
inputs.cc_note: Distinct from Original Balance (D13) — current UPB after any principal paydown. For new originations, current = original; servicer reports are not consumed by the codebase. Reclassify to Tier C; for new originations populator may copy D13 → D12 as a default with a flag.

inputs.label: Recourse flag
inputs.notes: Recourse Y/N from loan docs
inputs.cc_note: Reclassify to Tier C. (uw-intelligence.service.ts has a `recourse: boolean | null` field but that's a learned-rule attribute, not extraction.)

inputs.label: Note Date
inputs.notes: Original note date from loan docs
inputs.source: UnderwritingModel.loanDetails.originationDate (legacy ISO date string; pipeline.uwModel.loanDetails.originationDate in legacy POST)

inputs.label: Total Original Balance
inputs.notes: Original loan balance from loan docs

inputs.label: Cross-Collateralized
inputs.notes: Cross-collat Y/N from loan docs

inputs.label: Cash Management / Lockbox Type
inputs.notes: Hard / Soft / Springing lockbox from loan docs

inputs.label: Cash Flow Sweep Trigger
inputs.notes: Cash flow sweep trigger description from loan docs

inputs.label: Term (months)
inputs.notes: Loan term in MONTHS (not years) from loan docs. E15 holds months; formula in D15 converts to years for display.

inputs.label: ARD (Y/N)
inputs.notes: Anticipated Repayment Date Y/N from loan docs

inputs.label: Interest Only period (months)
inputs.notes: IO period in months from loan docs

inputs.label: Coupon
inputs.notes: Coupon rate as decimal (e.g., 0.07912 = 7.912%) from loan docs
inputs.cc_note: UNIT CONVERSION REQUIRED. Legacy UnderwritingModel.interestRate is stored as percent (e.g., 7.912) per ai-analysis.service.ts:714 comment. Excel cell expects decimal (0.07912). Divide by 100 at write time. The contract @cre/contracts.LoanTermsExtraction.interestRate is already 0..1 decimal — if/when the populator switches to graph records, drop the /100.

inputs.label: Total Sub Debt flag
inputs.notes: Has subordinate debt Y/N from loan docs

inputs.label: Control Pari status
inputs.notes: Whether this loan piece is the controlling or non-controlling note

inputs.label: Pari balance in trust
inputs.notes: Balance of pari piece in this trust

inputs.label: Controlling Party
inputs.notes: Name of controlling party. Often 'TBD' early in deal lifecycle.
inputs.cc_note: Often 'TBD' at underwriting time anyway — Tier C with default 'TBD' value is a reasonable populator behavior.

inputs.label: Senior Loan (Sources)
inputs.notes: Senior loan proceeds. NOTE: Original_Balance=82.46M but F27=63.93M, so this is the FUNDED amount at origination (different from current balance).
inputs.cc_note: Sources & Uses block (F27-F30) — no current extraction. Per Issue #35 item 10, S&U structured extraction is a planned engine extension.

inputs.label: Loan Purpose
inputs.notes: Refinance / Acquisition / Construction etc. from loan docs

inputs.label: Cash to Borrower
inputs.notes: Cash to borrower from sources & uses

inputs.label: Date Acquired
inputs.notes: Date sponsor acquired the property

inputs.label: Escrow / Reserves
inputs.notes: Up-front reserves established at closing

inputs.label: Purchase Price
inputs.notes: Purchase price. May be 'NAV' (Not Available) for old acquisitions where price isn't disclosed.

inputs.label: Closing Costs
inputs.notes: Closing costs from sources & uses

inputs.label: Total Cost Basis
inputs.notes: Total cost basis including improvements (acquisition + capex)

inputs.label: Subject Loan tag
inputs.notes: Loan type tag — Pari / Whole / Senior / Mezz etc.

inputs.label: Prior Sale Date
inputs.notes: Date of prior sale. Often the sponsor acquisition date for sponsor-acquired properties.

inputs.label: Prior Sale Price
inputs.notes: Prior sale price. May be NAV.

inputs.label: Occupied Units (template label drift?)
inputs.notes: INVESTIGATE — looks like a template label that Sunroad has filled with its own label text. May be label drift between blank and Sunroad. Verify during template cleanup.
inputs.cc_note: Cannot resolve without inspecting Blank_UW_Template_v2.xlsm at K85. If it's a label cell, strike from registry. If it's an input intended for MF/hotel occupancy, classify Tier A with source = PropertyMetadata.occupancyPhysical or rentRoll.summary.occupiedUnits.

Conclusions & Escrows.purpose: Concluded loan summary, supportable debt at refi, appraisal data, prior CMBS loan history, escrow structure.
Conclusions & Escrows.tier_summary: 17 inputs: 7 Tier A (appraisal value + prior CMBS history + appraisal line-items), 9 Tier B (concluded cap rate + escrow recommendations), 3 Tier C (comments/conclusion narrative), 1 X.

inputs.label: Concluded Cap Rate
inputs.notes: THE MOST CONSEQUENTIAL SINGLE JUDGMENT CELL in the template. LLM-with-handbook output, Milestone 2.

inputs.label: Appraisal Value
inputs.notes: From appraisal upload.
inputs.source: Analysis.extractionResult.fields.propertyValue.value (legacy @cre/shared ExtractionResult). Alternate: UnderwritingModel.impliedValue but that's derived, not the appraised value directly.
inputs.cc_note: The cleaner source is @cre/contracts.AppraisalExtraction.valueConclusion, but that's only present in the graph-pipeline ExtractionResult, not the legacy one in scope. The legacy `fields.propertyValue.value` is sourced from appraisal extraction during legacy pipeline run.

inputs.label: Prior CMBS Loan (Deal / Status)
inputs.notes: Prior CMBS deal name + delinquency status. Source: CMBS database lookup OR rating agency presale report.
inputs.cc_note: No CMBS database integration. Reclassify to Tier C.

inputs.label: Prior CMBS Loan Balance Before Disposition
inputs.notes: Same source as C18

inputs.label: Prior CMBS Trough NCF Year
inputs.notes: Same source as C18

inputs.label: Prior CMBS Trough NCF Amount
inputs.notes: Same source. Negative NCF here is notable — prior loan had a stressed period.

inputs.label: Comments
inputs.notes: Underwriter free-text comments. Red highlight.

inputs.label: Conclusion
inputs.notes: Underwriter free-text conclusion. Red highlight.

inputs.label: Real Estate Taxes — Per Appraisal
inputs.notes: From appraisal
inputs.cc_note: Appraisal extraction (both legacy and graph) captures only value/cap/methodology — no operating-statement line items. Reclassify to Tier C.

inputs.label: Real Estate Taxes — Up Front Deposit
inputs.notes: Underwritten reserve deposit. Judgment.

inputs.label: Insurance — Per Appraisal
inputs.notes: From appraisal
inputs.cc_note: Same reason as D47.

inputs.label: Insurance — Up Front Deposit
inputs.notes: Judgment

inputs.label: Replacement Reserves — Up Front
inputs.notes: Judgment. NOTE: 274758 = exactly the NRA, suggesting $1/SF formula rather than freeform judgment.

inputs.label: Replacement Reserves — Annual Escrow
inputs.notes: Annual replacement reserve. Judgment.

inputs.label: Immediate Repairs — Annual Escrow
inputs.notes: Judgment

inputs.label: General TI/LC — Up Front Deposit
inputs.notes: TI/LC reserve. Judgment.

inputs.label: Environmental Reserve (likely template label drift)
inputs.notes: Appears as label, not value. Verify during cleanup.
inputs.cc_note: Inspect Blank_UW_Template_v2.xlsm at E55 during cleanup. Likely a label cell; if so, strike from registry.

Property Detail - Comm.purpose: Office/Retail/Industrial property details. Asset-class-specific. Heavy formula computation from rent roll.
Property Detail - Comm.tier_summary: 17 inputs: 16 Tier A (property facts), 1 Tier C (demographic data needing external API).

inputs.label: Property Subtype
inputs.notes: Office subtype (CBD, Suburban, Medical, Flex). From ASR.

inputs.label: Surface Parking
inputs.notes: Surface parking space count from ASR
inputs.cc_note: Parking counts not in PropertyMetadata contract. Reclassify to Tier C.

inputs.label: Covered Parking
inputs.notes: Covered parking space count from ASR

inputs.label: Zoning Code
inputs.notes: Zoning designation from ASR
inputs.cc_note: Zoning not captured by PropertyMetadata. Reclassify to Tier C.

inputs.label: Zoning Description
inputs.notes: Plain-English description of zoning. May need to be derived from zoning code via lookup.

inputs.label: Number of Buildings
inputs.notes: From ASR

inputs.label: Land Area (acres)
inputs.notes: Land area in ACRES (not SF) from ASR
inputs.cc_note: Land area not captured by PropertyMetadata. Reclassify to Tier C.

inputs.label: Number of Stories
inputs.notes: From ASR

inputs.label: Ground Lease (Y/N)
inputs.notes: From ASR
inputs.cc_note: PropertyMetadata.ownershipInterest contains the answer indirectly ('Fee Simple' implies N), but the mapping is not categorical (e.g., 'Fee + Leasehold' is ambiguous). Reclassify to Tier C; the populator could heuristically default to 'N' when ownershipInterest==='Fee Simple' but this is a guess, not extraction.

inputs.label: Number of Outparcels
inputs.notes: 'NAP' = Not Applicable. Retail-specific concept; office uses NAP.
inputs.source: DERIVED: 'NAP' literal when Analysis.assetType !== 'Retail'; MISSING_FROM_CODEBASE_TIER_C when Retail
inputs.cc_note: Retail-specific. Populator writes literal 'NAP' for non-retail; for retail, no extraction exists — Tier C.

inputs.label: Ground Lease Subordinate
inputs.notes: If H12 (Ground Lease) = N, this = NAP
inputs.source: DERIVED: 'NAP' literal when H12 === 'N' (which it almost always is until ground-lease extraction exists)
inputs.cc_note: Conditional on H12 which is itself MISSING. In practice the populator can write 'NAP' as the default when no ground-lease data exists, matching Sunroad behavior.

inputs.label: Clear Height (ft)
inputs.notes: Industrial-specific concept. NAP for office. Important for industrial deals.
inputs.source: DERIVED: 'NAP' literal when Analysis.assetType !== 'Industrial'; MISSING_FROM_CODEBASE_TIER_C when Industrial
inputs.cc_note: Industrial-specific. Populator writes literal 'NAP' for non-industrial. Issue #35 lists clear height implicitly (P-IV-IND principles need it); future engine work will surface it.

inputs.label: Ground Lease Expiration Date
inputs.notes: If H12 = N, this = NAP

inputs.label: Building Class (duplicate)
inputs.notes: Duplicates Property & Loan Summary K6. Both should be populated from same source.

inputs.label: Ground Lease Options
inputs.notes: If H12 = N, this = NAP

inputs.label: GL Rent Steps
inputs.notes: If H12 = N, this = NAP

inputs.label: Average Home Price in Submarket
inputs.notes: Demographic data requiring external API. Tier C for Milestone 1.

Property Detail - Comm.non_input_notes: 204 formula cells compute the Rollover Summary from the Rent Roll.
Property Detail - Comm.convention_notes: NAP convention: when a field doesn't apply, populator writes 'NAP' rather than blank. Asset-class-specific.

Operating History and Pro Forma.purpose: Financial statements: 3 prior years + T-12 historical (Tier A), appraisal column (Tier A from appraisal), underwritten year-1 pro forma (Tier B), actual-in-place from rent roll (Tier A).

column_layout.L-M: Issuer UW — THE TIER B JUDGMENT COLUMN ($, $/measure)
column_layout.N-O: Actual Income In Place — from rent roll snapshot ($, $/measure)
column_layout.R: UW assumption notes / methodology comments (Tier B text)

Operating History and Pro Forma.tier_summary_v3_revised: ~51 Tier A cells originally. v3 finding: cols B/D/F (3rd/2nd/1st prior) cannot be populated today (Issue #38 — no per-period extraction). Col J (Appraisal) cannot be populated today (appraisal extraction has no operating-statement line items). Only col H (T-12) and col N (actual-in-place) are populatable from current legacy POST records. ~9 cells map cleanly; ~36 cells reclassify to Tier C until Issues #38 and appraisal-extraction-expansion ship.

column_B_D_F.blocker_issue: #38 (open) — per-period array extraction from seller UW models
column_B_D_F.explanation: Current extraction is single-period (T-12 from ASR or Seller UW column). Multi-year historical arrays are not parsed.

column_H_T12.source_template: pipeline.uwModelFromAsr.income.{grossPotentialRent | otherIncome} OR pipeline.uwModelFromAsr.expenses.{realEstateTaxes | insurance | utilities | repairsAndMaintenance | management | generalAndAdmin | payroll | replacementReserves}
column_H_T12.explanation: uwModelFromAsr is the per-source ASR extraction surfaced for Operating History column rendering (per underwriting-pipeline.service.ts:53 comment 'surfaced so downstream consumers (template populator) can render the multi-period Operating History columns')

per_cell_mapping.H11 (Bad Debt Expense): MISSING_FROM_CODEBASE_TIER_C — legacy ExpenseSection has no badDebt field. Sometimes captured as a vacancyLoss subset; not separately extracted.
per_cell_mapping.H15 (Expense Reimbursements): MISSING_FROM_CODEBASE_TIER_C — legacy IncomeSection doesn't separately track reimbursements; sometimes embedded in otherIncome.
per_cell_mapping.H6 (Average Physical Occupancy): PropertyMetadata.occupancyPhysical (fraction; multiply by 100 if cell expects percent)

column_J_appraisal.blocker: Appraisal extraction (both @cre/contracts.AppraisalExtraction and the legacy bag) carries only {valueConclusion, capRate, methodology} — no income/expense line items. Reclassify all col J line-item rows to Tier C.
column_J_appraisal.exception: J6/J7 occupancy MIGHT be derivable from appraisal narrative if extraction expands; today MISSING.

column_L_M_underwriting_judgment.note: Per registry, col L is the Tier B judgment column — LLM-with-handbook output. The graph pipeline DOES produce AdjustedInputs.income/expenses but the legacy POST doesn't have AdjustedInputs in scope. When new-spine populator ships, AdjustedInputs.income.* and AdjustedInputs.expenses.* will be the mapping for these cells.

column_N_O_actual_in_place.source_template: Derived from pipeline.rentRoll (the contract RentRoll record built at line 949-981)

per_cell_mapping.N (Actual In Place income lines): DERIVED: sum across rentRoll.units of (baseRentMonthly * 12) for occupied units; can also aggregate via existing rent roll summarization code
per_cell_mapping.occupancy stats (L6/L7): DERIVED: rentRoll.summary.occupiedUnits / rentRoll.summary.totalUnits for physical; rentRoll.summary.economicOccupancy for economic — but these are Tier B per registry (UW column), not Tier A

column_N_O_actual_in_place.cc_note: Actual-in-place column is supposed to come from rent roll snapshot. Current rent roll extraction is unit-level; aggregation to line-item bucketing (GPR, other income, reimbursements) requires aggregation logic that doesn't exist today. Recommend implementing an aggregator OR reclassifying col N to Tier C until aggregator ships.

Q_R_columns.note: Growth rate parameters and UW notes — LLM-with-handbook output.

sources_v3.H11: MISSING_FROM_CODEBASE_TIER_C (legacy ExpenseSection has no badDebt field)

sources_v3.H15: MISSING_FROM_CODEBASE_TIER_C (legacy IncomeSection has no reimbursements field)

critical_observations: v3: 30+ Tier A cells in columns B/D/F cannot be populated until Issue #38 (per-period extraction) ships.
v3: 11+ Tier A cells in column J cannot be populated until appraisal extraction expands to line-item operating statement.
v3: ~9 Tier A cells in column H ARE populatable from pipeline.uwModelFromAsr. This is the practical Milestone 1 scope for this sheet.
Column L (UW year-1) remains Tier B per Milestone 2 scope.
If appraisal upload is not currently in the upload set, all column J cells become Tier C (red) for Milestone 1.

369 formula cells compute EGI/Total Op Ex/NOI/NCF/DSCR from the 74 inputs. Getting the column H inputs right makes a partial sheet work; col B/D/F/J/L blank is acceptable for an underwriter to fill manually.

Property Detail - MF SS MHP.milestone_1_status: DEFERRED to v1.5 — no example data.

10 Yr Pro Forma.purpose: 10-year projection grid. Almost entirely formula-driven from year-1.
10 Yr Pro Forma.tier_summary: TIER B sheet. The 2 hardcoded cells are likely growth-rate parameters.

Rent Roll.purpose: Tenant-by-tenant rent roll. Existing rent roll extraction pipeline populates this.

structural_notes_v3.current_populator: apps/api/src/services/template-engine.service.ts:914 — populateRentRollTab(worksheet, options.rentRoll) handles this in legacy POST today

per_tenant_field_mapping.D (Sq. Ft.): rentRoll.lines[].squareFeet (verify field name in RentRoll contract; the registry's RentRoll.units shape from @cre/contracts.extraction has unitId/tenantName/leaseStart/leaseEnd/baseRentMonthly etc.)
per_tenant_field_mapping.Others (CAM, Recovery, etc.): Partial coverage from contract RentRoll; full mapping to be verified during integration

Rent Roll.milestone_1_status: DEFER detailed cell inventory. Existing populator covers most of this; verify column-by-column mapping during integration.

Presentation Rent Roll.purpose: Print-formatted version of Rent Roll. Auto-derives via formulas.

Borrower.milestone_1_status: Mix of Tier A (sponsor name from Analysis.extractionResult.descriptors.sponsorName.value or .borrowerName) and Tier C (financial statements).

Market.purpose: Market and submarket data — vacancy, rents, absorption.
Market.milestone_1_status: Tier C for Milestone 1. Could be Tier A in future via existing research.service.js (searchMarket).

next_steps_for_cc_integration_v3.1_milestone_1_scope_recheck: Given ~65% of Tier A cells are MISSING_FROM_CODEBASE, decide whether (a) reduce Milestone 1 to the ~35 cells that ARE mappable + everything else red-highlighted Tier C, OR (b) expand pre-Milestone-1 extraction to fill the biggest gaps (loan structural terms, sources & uses, multi-period historicals). Recommend (a).
next_steps_for_cc_integration_v3.2_projector_service_v3_input_shape: If proceeding with Milestone 1: build a projector service with signature roughly: projectCellWrites({propertyMetadata, uwModel, uwModelFromAsr, extractionResultLegacy, rentRoll, analysisAssetType, dealControlNumber?}): CellWrite[]. All inputs available in legacy POST today.
next_steps_for_cc_integration_v3.3_template_writer_unchanged: The existing populateTemplate machinery (template-engine.service.ts:894) already handles workbook open + cell write + style application + formula preservation. Replace its label-scanner with the new registry-driven projector output.
next_steps_for_cc_integration_v3.4_unit_conversions_to_handle: (a) UnderwritingModel.interestRate is percent → divide by 100 for D18 Coupon. (b) PropertyMetadata.occupancyPhysical is fraction 0..1 — confirm template H6/H7 cell format; multiply by 100 if cell expects percent.
next_steps_for_cc_integration_v3.5_NAP_convention_implementation: Populator must dispatch by Analysis.assetType to write 'NAP' for cross-asset-class non-applicable cells (C13/C14 in Property Detail - Comm). Centralize the dispatch in a single helper.
6_open_questions_for_user: Should M2 Cover Page Deal Title be reclassified to FORMULA (don't write; let template's M2 formula derive from H2)?
Should the populator copy D13 Original Balance → D12 Current Balance as a default for new originations (with a flag), or leave D12 Tier C?
For Property Detail - Comm cells that are NAP-by-asset-class (clear height for office, outparcels for office), should the populator write 'NAP' explicitly or leave blank?
Should the populator's gap report (per-deal) surface to the underwriter at the top of the populated workbook so they know which red-highlighted cells need manual entry?
